reasoning: Friedrich Merz took office as the German Chancellor on May 6, 2025. Although his election took a second round due to coalition wavering, he has successfully served in the position during the months following. A review of news up to the knowledge cutoff (November 9, 2025) reveals no announcements of resignation, failing health, successful votes of no confidence, or sudden coalition collapse. The market traded at ~6% probability as of late October. Merz has continued fulfilling regular duties (such as the October 23 European Council meeting) and there is no indication he will step down before the end of the year. Given that German Chancellors rarely resign within their first year without extreme external shocks or severe illness—and only ~7 weeks remain in 2025—the probability is conservatively set at 5%.
